controls.threat: Unauthorized runtime API calls with over-scoped credentials.
controls.failure_mode: Scope validation weakens and unauthorized calls are accepted.
controls.expected_remediation: Fail release lane, restore strict scope policy, and rerun API abuse regression checks.
controls.success_criterion: 100% of runtime API requests with invalid or over-scoped tokens are rejected in CI abuse tests.

controls.threat: Unsigned or tampered policy/provenance artifacts entering release and promotion paths.
controls.failure_mode: Signature or attestation verification is bypassed for release artifacts.
controls.expected_remediation: Block promotion, reissue signatures/attestations, and investigate key or policy tamper.
controls.success_criterion: All required signature and attestation gates pass with zero bypass findings in release lanes.

controls.threat: Security scanning or dependency controls bypassed due to workflow/toolchain drift.
controls.failure_mode: Unpinned or drifted security tools execute, reducing scan reliability.
controls.expected_remediation: Restore pinned toolchain state, verify dependency provenance, and rerun security scans.
controls.success_criterion: Toolchain integrity and dependency provenance gates pass with no drift findings per run.

controls.threat: Evidence omission/tamper in archived security outputs.
controls.failure_mode: Evidence artifacts are missing, altered, or unsigned while checks still report success.
controls.expected_remediation: Halt publish path, rebuild evidence from verified run inputs, and re-sign integrity artifacts.
controls.success_criterion: Evidence integrity gates pass and every required artifact is present, signed, and hash-verifiable.
